name: Western Union
description_short: Pittsburgh Western Union at 4885 McKnight Rd offers check cashing services with extended hours open seven days a week.
description_long:
Western Union at 4885 McKnight Rd in Pittsburgh, PA is a standalone check-cashing location open seven days a week from 8 AM to 9 PM. The location sits on McKnight Road, a major commercial corridor in Pittsburgh, making it accessible for residents across the city's north and east sides. Extended evening and weekend hours serve Pittsburgh residents who need check cashing outside typical banking windows.

This Pittsburgh Western Union location handles check cashing for payroll checks, government benefits, insurance settlements, and third-party checks. You can reach the store at +1 412-366-6359 to confirm specific document requirements or ask about any restrictions on check amounts. The staff can typically process your check while you wait.

Pittsburgh residents should bring a valid government-issued ID and the check you want cashed. Western Union is particularly useful for those without a bank account or needing quick cash access outside banking hours. The 4885 McKnight Rd location serves Pittsburgh's North Shore and surrounding neighborhoods with reliable weekday and weekend service.
pros: Extensive physical agent network with 150+ locations across US, embedded in major retailers like Walmart, Safeway, and City Market for convenient access
24/7 online service via WU.com for initiating transfers, complemented by in-store options during agent hours
Real-time transfer tracking capability allows customers to monitor transaction status
Multiple service options including wire transfers, money orders, bill payments, mobile top-up, and inmate payments in one platform
Rewards program and referral incentives for repeat customers
Accepts various payment methods and supports multiple languages (English, Spanish, Haitian Creole, Chinese, French, Vietnamese)
Established global brand with decades of operational history and trust
cons: No fee schedule or pricing information displayed on website—customers must use 'Estimate price' tool and cannot compare costs upfront
Limited transparency on exchange rates for international transfers or exact costs for domestic services
Website content is primarily transactional navigation without educational resources on how fees work or what to expect
Some locations limited to 'Money Order Services only' (e.g., Circle K in Leadville), reducing service availability at certain agents
Orange wallet payment method appears to have technical issues ('temporarily unavailable') suggesting service reliability concerns
best_for: Customers needing immediate cash pickup from a physical location with flexible weekend/evening hours
People sending money domestically or internationally who value brand recognition and established trust
Users who prefer in-store transactions over fully digital services and want to avoid online-only providers
Customers seeking a hybrid option combining online convenience with physical agent access for added security
diagnosis: Western Union is best for consumers who need accessible money transfer or cash services through physical locations and value an established brand, but pricing opacity makes it essential to use their 'Estimate price' tool before committing to any transaction. The lack of transparent fee disclosure on their website is a significant caveat compared to competitors.
meta_description: Western Union at 4885 McKnight Rd, Pittsburgh, PA. Check cashing open daily 8AM–9PM.
